title: Gymnogramma tenera
intro: Gymnogramma tenera, also known as Gymnogramma, is a type of fern plant that belongs to the family Pteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Gymnogramma tenera include sesquiterpenoids and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Gymnogramma is commonly prepared in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as being used in various herbal formulations to alleviate respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Gymnogramma tenera is classified as a species of fern in the family Blechnaceae. It belongs to the genus Gymnogramma and the kingdom Plantae. This plant is native to various parts of the world, including Australia and parts of Asia. The Blechnaceae family is a group of ferns commonly known as the chain ferns. Gymnogramma tenera is a type of epiphytic fern that grows on other plants or trees.
